stem: An engineer takes 15 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 197.4 % and a standard deviation of 28.5 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically overestimates the true value by between 4.6% and 10.2%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 197.4 %. After correcting for the 7.4% bias: ≈ 183.8 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.6%-10.2%) and sampling variability: [164.4, 203.4]. Point estimate: 183.8 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
